data.title: Stuart Cooper suspends Congressional campaign, endorses Matt Van Epps for Congress, TN-07 in 2025.
data.description: In Franklin, Tennessee on October 3, 2025, it was announced that a candidate for the United States Congress has decided to withdraw their candidacy. The individual expressed deep reflection on the decision before stepping back from the race. They have urged their supporters to redirect their votes towards another candidate, Matt Van Epps, highlighting their acquaintance with him over the past few months as a reason for this endorsement.

data.title: California grants gig drivers the right to unionize, impacting thousands in the state. #gigworkers #laborrights
data.description: A recent study showed that over 60% of households in the United States have at least one pet, with dogs being the most popular choice among pet owners. The study also revealed that there are approximately 90 million pet dogs in the country, outnumbering pet cats by a significant margin. Furthermore, the research indicated that pet ownership has been on the rise in recent years, with millennials being the demographic group most likely to own a pet. The study suggests that the bond between humans and pets continues to strengthen, leading to an increase in pet ownership across the nation.

data.title: Free webinars by Consolidated Credit this fall offer tips on budgeting for homeownership, holiday spending survival, and achieving financial and mental wellness.
data.description: A reputable financial education and counseling organization, Consolidated Credit, based in FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla., has announced a series of free monthly webinars aimed at educating consumers on crucial financial topics. These webinars will cover essential subjects such as budgeting for homeownership, navigating finances during the holiday season, and strategies for achieving financial stability and mental well-being. The first webinar is scheduled for Oct. 3, 2025. Interested individuals can join these informative sessions online to enhance their financial literacy.

data.title: Special screenings of "A Life Among Elephants" in NY and LA celebrate Dr. Iain Douglas-Hamilton's legacy at Save the Elephants.
data.description: Save the Elephants, an international nonprofit organization committed to ensuring a future for elephants, has revealed plans for two exclusive showings of A Life Among Elephants. This captivating movie follows the journey of founder Dr. Iain Douglas-Hamilton and his impactful efforts. Set to take place in both New York and Los Angeles on October 3, 2025, these screenings promise to shed light on the important work being done to protect these magnificent creatures.

data.title: Creator of ICE tracking app, removed by Apple and Google under federal pressure, plans legal action. #LegalAction #ICETracking #AppDeveloper
data.description: After facing pressure from the federal government, Apple and Google have removed ICE tracking apps from their platforms. The decision comes as a response to concerns raised about the implications of these apps. One determined developer is refusing to back down and is gearing up to challenge this move.

data.title: Lawsuit against Yuga Labs dismissed after failing to prove Bored Ape Yacht Club NFTs meet SEC's Howey Test.
data.description: The plaintiffs were unable to demonstrate how Bored Ape Yacht Club and other non-fungible tokens (NFTs) are considered investment contracts according to the Securities and Exchange Commission's three-pronged Howey Test.

data.title: CleanSpark sells $48.7M in Bitcoin; now holds over 13K BTC in treasury. Stock up 5% on increased production in September.
data.description: CleanSpark, a miner listed on the Nasdaq, saw a more than 5% increase in shares on Friday following a report of increased production in September.
